title: XOR swap: exchange two values with no temporary
statement_informal: The three-XOR trick (a^=b; b^=a; a^=b) swaps two values with no temporary variable. xorswap a b = (b, a), proved purely from XOR being commutative, associative and self-cancelling (x XOR x = 0).

cost_model.notes: Three XORs, no temporary. Correctness from Nat.lxor_comm / Nat.lxor_assoc / Nat.lxor_nilpotent / Nat.lxor_0_l.

references: The classic in-place XOR swap; correctness rests on the group structure of bitwise XOR.
